quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Medical, Environmental, and Analytical Applications
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Respiration Stoichiometry, Gas Volumes, Titration Calculations, Purity Analysis, Biochemical Reactions

questions.question:
If a person consumes 100 g of glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆), what mass of CO₂ is produced by complete aerobic respiration?

Equation: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O
questions.explanation:
Molar mass glucose = 180.16 g/mol
Moles glucose = 100/180.16 = 0.555 mol
Mole ratio glucose:CO₂ = 1:6
Moles CO₂ = 0.555 × 6 = 3.33 mol
Molar mass CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Mass CO₂ = 3.33 × 44.01 = 147 g

questions.question:
A resting adult inhales 0.50 L of air per breath (21% O₂ by volume). How many moles of O₂ are inhaled in a single breath at 25°C and 1 atm?

(Molar volume at 25°C = 24.5 L/mol)
questions.explanation:
Volume O₂ per breath = 0.50 L × 0.21 = 0.105 L
At 25°C, molar volume = 24.5 L/mol
Moles O₂ = 0.105 / 24.5 = 0.00429 mol

questions.question: Urea, (NH₂)₂CO, is the waste product of protein metabolism. If 15 g of urea is excreted, how many moles of nitrogen were removed from the body?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass urea = 60.06 g/mol
Moles urea = 15/60.06 = 0.250 mol
Each urea molecule contains 2 N atoms
Moles N = 0.250 × 2 = 0.500 mol

questions.question: The hydrolysis of ATP releases energy. If 0.010 moles of ATP are hydrolyzed, and the molar mass is 507.2 g/mol, what mass of ATP was used?

The liver oxidizes ethanol at a rate of roughly 10 g per hour. How many moles of O₂ are required to fully oxidize 10 g of C₂H₅OH to CO₂ and H₂O?

Equation: C₂H₅OH + 3O₂ → 2CO₂ + 3H₂O
questions.explanation:
Molar mass ethanol = 46.07 g/mol
Moles ethanol = 10/46.07 = 0.217 mol
Mole ratio ethanol:O₂ = 1:3
Moles O₂ = 0.217 × 3 = 0.651 mol

questions.question:
Some high-strength antacids use Al(OH)₃. If a tablet contains 0.500 g of aluminum hydroxide, what volume of 0.1 M HCl can it neutralize?

Equation: Al(OH)₃ + 3HCl → AlCl₃ + 3H₂O
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Al(OH)₃ = 78.00 g/mol
Moles Al(OH)₃ = 0.500/78.00 = 0.00641 mol
Mole ratio Al(OH)₃:HCl = 1:3
Moles HCl = 0.00641 × 3 = 0.01923 mol
Volume HCl = 0.01923/0.1 = 0.1923 L = 192 mL

questions.question:
To treat acid indigestion, a patient swallows 1.20 g of NaHCO₃. Determine the volume of 0.12 M HCl required to react completely with the baking soda.

Equation: NaHCO₃ + HCl → NaCl + H₂O + CO₂
questions.explanation:
Molar mass NaHCO₃ = 84.01 g/mol
Moles NaHCO₃ = 1.20/84.01 = 0.01428 mol
Mole ratio NaHCO₃:HCl = 1:1
Moles HCl = 0.01428 mol
Volume HCl = 0.01428/0.12 = 0.119 L = 119 mL

questions.question:
A generic 0.750 g antacid tablet is 85% CaCO₃ by mass. How many milliliters of 0.20 M HCl are needed to react with the active ingredient?

Equation: CaCO₃ + 2HCl → CaCl₂ + H₂O + CO₂
questions.explanation:
Mass pure CaCO₃ = 0.750 × 0.85 = 0.6375 g
Molar mass CaCO₃ = 100.09 g/mol
Moles CaCO₃ = 0.6375/100.09 = 0.00637 mol
Mole ratio CaCO₃:HCl = 1:2
Moles HCl = 0.00637 × 2 = 0.01274 mol
Volume HCl = 0.01274/0.20 = 0.0637 L = 63.7 mL

questions.question:
A 25.0 mL sample of rainwater is collected near a coal plant. To neutralize the sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) present, 4.5 mL of 0.0050 M NaOH is required. Determine the molarity of the acid in the rain.

Equation: H₂SO₄ + 2NaOH → Na₂SO₄ + 2H₂O
questions.explanation:
Moles NaOH = 0.0045 × 0.0050 = 2.25 × 10⁻⁵ mol
Mole ratio NaOH:H₂SO₄ = 2:1
Moles H₂SO₄ = 2.25 × 10⁻⁵/2 = 1.125 × 10⁻⁵ mol
Volume rainwater = 0.0250 L
Molarity H₂SO₄ = 1.125 × 10⁻⁵/0.0250 = 4.5 × 10⁻⁴ M

questions.question:
A 5.00 mL sample of white vinegar (acetic acid, HC₂H₃O₂) requires 35.2 mL of 0.125 M NaOH for complete neutralization. Calculate the molarity of the acetic acid.

Equation: HC₂H₃O₂ + NaOH → NaC₂H₃O₂ + H₂O
questions.explanation:
Moles NaOH = 0.0352 × 0.125 = 0.00440 mol
Mole ratio 1:1, so moles acetic acid = 0.00440 mol
Volume vinegar = 0.00500 L
Molarity = 0.00440/0.00500 = 0.880 M

questions.question:
A 10.0 mL liquid extract from boiled spinach contains Fe²⁺. It is titrated with 0.0200 M potassium permanganate (KMnO₄). If 6.4 mL is required for the reaction, what is the concentration of iron in the extract?

Equation: 5Fe²⁺ + MnO₄⁻ + 8H⁺ → 5Fe³⁺ + Mn²⁺ + 4H₂O
questions.explanation:
Moles MnO₄⁻ = 0.0064 × 0.0200 = 1.28 × 10⁻⁴ mol
Mole ratio Fe²⁺:MnO₄⁻ = 5:1
Moles Fe²⁺ = 1.28 × 10⁻⁴ × 5 = 6.4 × 10⁻⁴ mol
Volume extract = 0.0100 L
Molarity Fe²⁺ = 6.4 × 10⁻⁴/0.0100 = 0.0640 M

questions.question:
A 50.0 mL sample of pool water is tested for 'free chlorine' by reacting it with 0.010 M sodium thiosulfate (Na₂S₂O₃). If 3.2 mL of the thiosulfate is used, what is the molarity of the chlorine in the pool?

Equation: Cl₂ + 2Na₂S₂O₃ → 2NaCl + Na₂S₄O₆
questions.explanation:
Moles Na₂S₂O₃ = 0.0032 × 0.010 = 3.2 × 10⁻⁵ mol
Mole ratio Cl₂:S₂O₃²⁻ = 1:2
Moles Cl₂ = 3.2 × 10⁻⁵/2 = 1.6 × 10⁻⁵ mol
Volume pool sample = 0.0500 L
Molarity Cl₂ = 1.6 × 10⁻⁵/0.0500 = 3.2 × 10⁻⁴ M

questions.question:
A 15.0 mL solution derived from dissolving a silver alloy reacts with 24.5 mL of 0.150 M NaCl. Calculate the concentration of silver ions (Ag⁺) in the original 15.0 mL solution.

Equation: Ag⁺ + Cl⁻ → AgCl
questions.explanation:
Moles NaCl = 0.0245 × 0.150 = 0.003675 mol
Mole ratio Ag⁺:Cl⁻ = 1:1
Moles Ag⁺ = 0.003675 mol
Volume silver solution = 0.0150 L
Molarity Ag⁺ = 0.003675/0.0150 = 0.245 M

questions.question:
A 4.50 g sample of 'dirty' magnesium metal reacts with excess hydrochloric acid to produce 3.85 g of magnesium chloride (MgCl₂). Calculate the percentage purity of the magnesium sample.

Equation: Mg + 2HCl → MgCl₂ + H₂
questions.explanation:
Molar mass MgCl₂ = 95.21 g/mol
Moles MgCl₂ = 3.85/95.21 = 0.04045 mol
Mole ratio Mg:MgCl₂ = 1:1
Moles pure Mg = 0.04045 mol
Molar mass Mg = 24.31 g/mol
Mass pure Mg = 0.04045 × 24.31 = 0.983 g
Percentage purity = (0.983/4.50) × 100% = 21.8%

questions.question:
A 10.00 g sample of impure copper(II) oxide (CuO) is reacted with excess sulfuric acid. If 15.20 g of copper(II) sulfate (CuSO₄) is recovered from the solution, what is the percentage purity of the oxide?

Equation: CuO + H₂SO₄ → CuSO₄ + H₂O
questions.explanation:
Molar mass CuSO₄ = 159.61 g/mol
Moles CuSO₄ = 15.20/159.61 = 0.09524 mol
Mole ratio CuO:CuSO₄ = 1:1
Moles pure CuO = 0.09524 mol
Molar mass CuO = 79.55 g/mol
Mass pure CuO = 0.09524 × 79.55 = 7.58 g
Percentage purity = (7.58/10.00) × 100% = 75.8%

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered real-world stoichiometry applications! 🧪⚕️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review unit conversions and mole calculations in medical contexts.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting between mass, moles, and concentration correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review stoichiometric calculations, molar mass determinations, and concentration conversions.
